Chronosynclastic Infundibula

A place where all the different kinds of truths fit together.

they sowed their isn't they reaped their same sun moon stars rain
E.E. Cummings
The only thing I ever learned is that some people are lucky and some people aren't and not even a graduate of the Harvard Business School can say why.
Kurt Vonnegut, The Sirens of Titan
I knew that nothing stranger had ever happened, that nothing stranger could ever happen. Why should I be my aunt, or me, or anyone?
Elizabeth Bishop, In the Waiting Room
What similarities-- boots, hands, the family voice I felt in my throat... held us all together or made us all just one? How-I didn't know any word for it-how unlikely...
Elizabeth Bishop, In the Waiting Room
So it goes.
Kurt Vonnegut, Slaughterhouse-Five
You realize that nature is ruthless and our existence is very fragile, temporary, and precious. But to go on with your daily affairs, you can't really think about that...which is probably why everyone takes the world for granted and why we act so thoughtlessly. It's very confusing. I suppose it will all make sense when we grow up.
Bill Watterson, Calvin & Hobbes
We have a right to know what's going on!
Kurt Vonnegut, The Sirens of Titan
If it runs and seems correct, you ship it. It's good enough
a sarcastic Software Engineering professor
In real life, everything you do can and probably will fail.
that same Software Engineering professor
There's who you are, and there's who you think you are, and your personal misery is defined by how big the gap is between them.
Glee
The greatest hazard of all, losing one's self, can happen very quietly in the world, as though it were nothing at all.
Soren Kierkegaard
posts courses projects chronosynclastic infundibula about
